weeks.label: Week of Jun 22–28, 2026
weeks.quote: AI adoption in L&D just passed 87% — but confidence in what to build next hasn't caught up.

methods.text: Remove environment-setup friction (Codespaces) instead of running a training program
Reframe the goal from "adoption" to "frequent, effective use" (12+ days/month), backed by 2-day workshops + recurring office hours
Pair new hires with a mentor on real backlog tasks for 3-4 weeks instead of toy onboarding projects
Build a retrieval layer over existing Slack/runbooks/PR history instead of writing new onboarding content
methods.source: Lemonade Angle, via Bersin's Dynamic Enablement model

methods.text: Mine real work-sample signals (GitHub activity, review patterns) instead of self-reported skills surveys

doThis: Build the "why do you trust this?" evaluation habit directly into the workflow (e.g. a PR template field), not a separate training module
Mine real behavioral signals (GitHub activity, usage data) for skills/mobility data instead of asking people to self-report
Teach AI fluency (using AI for real advantage) — most 2026 programs stop at literacy (safe baseline use), which is the wrong finish line
avoidThis: Don't assume tool access equals adoption — Booking.com's first AI rollout stalled until they added real enablement
Don't let AI's confident tone substitute for evaluation — Wharton found people accepted wrong AI answers 80% of the time, confidently
Don't repeat a secondhand stat without checking the primary source — a widely-shared Mastercard figure didn't match its own case study
